fields.summary: Normalize unit of resource objects in RM to avoid unit conversion in critical path

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

issuetype.description: An improvement or enhancement to an existing feature or task.
issuetype.name: Improvement

fields.description: Currently resource conversion could happen in critical code path when different unit is specified by client. This could impact performance and throughput of RM a lot. We should do unit normalization when resource passed to RM and avoid expensive unit conversion every time.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

fields.summary: ClusterNodeTracker maximum allocation does not respect resource units

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A problem which impairs or prevents the functions of the product.
issuetype.name: Bug

comments.body:
[~wangda] [~sunilg] Thanks for giving me an opportunity to contribute to this JIRA.

Had a offline discussion with [~sunilg] on the approaches. Attached a patch to convert resource value if its units is different from the corresponding resource units at RM side as part of resource object creation so that units conversions happening at several other places can be removed to improve performance.

Thanks [~manirajv06@gmail.com] for working on this. 

I agree with what [~sunilg] suggested. Instead of doing this on RM's deserialization part, we should do this in ResourceInformation#newInstance. The reason is: {{Resources}} class by definition, can be used by map reduce as well. It will not be enough to only change RM's deserialization path and remove all unit conversions in Resources.

I also agree that changing {{setUnits}} to check unit might be a perf issue since it is invoked by {{copy}}. Can we add an internal {{setUnitsWithoutChecking}} method, which will be used by {{copy}}, and when {{setUnits}} is invoked, instead of setting unit, we will change value. For example, assume a resource information's value is 100, unit is MB (which is same as {{ResourceUtils.getUnitsOfResourceXXX}}), if user calls setUnit(GB). Instead of changing unit to GB, we will keep the unit to MB and change value to 100 * 1024. And we can avoid the expensive {{setUnit}} in all RM's code paths.

Thoughts?

bq.Based on my understaning, it has two parts. 1) Changes in ResourceInformation#copy 2)Changes in ResourceInformation#setUnits. #2 changes would be something like

I think ResourceInformation#copy could invoke ResourceInformation#setUnitsWithoutChecking.
Then ResourceInformation#setUnits could be similar to  your code snipper which u gave above.

[~sunilg] Thanks for your comments.

Had an offline discussion with [~sunilg]. Summary is, Earlier proposal leads to cyclic dependency issues between RI and ResourceUtils class and can run in infinite loop while creating objects. Hence, decided to use {{setUnitsWithoutValidation}} in {{ResourceInformation#newInstance}} method and do the units conversion in {{ResourcePBImpl#initResources}} if there is any difference. Also, to take up setUnits() optimization as next step. Attached patch for the same.

Reading resources info from resource-types.xml had some issues as it has been placed in incorrect path only for the modules other than hadoop-yarn-common. This fix, for each clients (for ex, yarn rm etc) test case execution (wherever it is required), it create the same file and place in client's target directory, so that it can read to populate resource type info and delete the same at the end of test case execution. As a long term, we will need to make this change as common and for every resources, not just for copying resource-types-4.xml as there would be test cases coming in future where in it may required to copy the resources for its execution.

[~wangda] [~sunilg] Thanks for giving me an opportunity to contribute to this JIRA.

Had a offline discussion with [~sunilg] on the approaches. Attached a patch to convert resource value if its units is different from the corresponding resource units at RM side as part of resource object creation so that units conversions happening at several other places can be removed to improve performance.

Thanks [~manirajv06@gmail.com] for working on this. 

I agree with what [~sunilg] suggested. Instead of doing this on RM's deserialization part, we should do this in ResourceInformation#newInstance. The reason is: {{Resources}} class by definition, can be used by map reduce as well. It will not be enough to only change RM's deserialization path and remove all unit conversions in Resources.

I also agree that changing {{setUnits}} to check unit might be a perf issue since it is invoked by {{copy}}. Can we add an internal {{setUnitsWithoutChecking}} method, which will be used by {{copy}}, and when {{setUnits}} is invoked, instead of setting unit, we will change value. For example, assume a resource information's value is 100, unit is MB (which is same as {{ResourceUtils.getUnitsOfResourceXXX}}), if user calls setUnit(GB). Instead of changing unit to GB, we will keep the unit to MB and change value to 100 * 1024. And we can avoid the expensive {{setUnit}} in all RM's code paths.

Thoughts?

bq.Based on my understaning, it has two parts. 1) Changes in ResourceInformation#copy 2)Changes in ResourceInformation#setUnits. #2 changes would be something like

I think ResourceInformation#copy could invoke ResourceInformation#setUnitsWithoutChecking.
Then ResourceInformation#setUnits could be similar to  your code snipper which u gave above.

[~sunilg] Thanks for your comments.

Had an offline discussion with [~sunilg]. Summary is, Earlier proposal leads to cyclic dependency issues between RI and ResourceUtils class and can run in infinite loop while creating objects. Hence, decided to use {{setUnitsWithoutValidation}} in {{ResourceInformation#newInstance}} method and do the units conversion in {{ResourcePBImpl#initResources}} if there is any difference. Also, to take up setUnits() optimization as next step. Attached patch for the same.

Reading resources info from resource-types.xml had some issues as it has been placed in incorrect path only for the modules other than hadoop-yarn-common. This fix, for each clients (for ex, yarn rm etc) test case execution (wherever it is required), it create the same file and place in client's target directory, so that it can read to populate resource type info and delete the same at the end of test case execution. As a long term, we will need to make this change as common and for every resources, not just for copying resource-types-4.xml as there would be test cases coming in future where in it may required to copy the resources for its execution.
